hero.title: Scheduling primitives for shadcn apps that need real interaction.
hero.body: CalendarCN gives you a reusable scheduler with month, week, day, and agenda views, recurring events, resource lanes, drag-and-drop editing, resize handles, and typed callbacks you can wire into your own state and persistence.

snippet.title: Clone the starter and make it yours
snippet.body: CalendarCN ships as a starter today rather than a registry package. Pull the repo, install dependencies, and wire the exported calendar primitives into your own data, permissions, and persistence layer.

points: Month, week, day, and agenda views on one event model
Recurring events, resources, drag, resize, and selection built in
Token-driven styling that fits the rest of your shadcn app

snapshot.title: A scheduler that fits inside an existing product.
snapshot.body: Use the full CalendarRoot surface or compose the exported views yourself. The same event and resource contracts power both the demo and the library.

components.title: Composable building blocks for shipping scheduling UI.
components.body: CalendarCN is structured as a reusable library. Use the full scheduler shell or pull in the toolbar, individual views, types, and slots where they fit.

items.name: CalendarRoot
items.title: The orchestrator for the full schedule
items.body: It coordinates visible range, recurrence expansion, selection, drag and resize interactions, and view rendering from one controlled API.
items.detail: Use it when you want a complete scheduler surface without moving persistence or business rules into the component.

items.name: CalendarToolbar
items.title: Navigation wired to calendar state
items.body: Previous, next, today, and view switching already line up with the calendar API, so you are not rebuilding basic controls around every embed.
items.detail: Keep the default toolbar or restyle it to match the surrounding app shell.

items.name: Views
items.title: Month, week, day, and agenda
items.body: Each view reads the same event and resource contracts, so you can change density and planning mode without changing your data layer.
items.detail: That makes it practical to ship a broad planner, a focused day view, and an agenda feed from one model.

items.name: Types + slots
items.title: Typed contracts and slot-level customization
items.body: Events, resources, recurrences, and mutation payloads are typed, and the component exposes className slots for the key calendar surfaces.
items.detail: You keep the interaction model, then adapt the styling and rendering to your own product.

capabilities.title: Built for schedules that keep changing.
capabilities.body: A useful calendar has to do more than render boxes. CalendarCN ships the interactions and state contracts you need once users start moving work around.

items.title: One contract across every view
items.body: Month, week, day, and agenda all consume the same event shape, so your scheduling data does not fork as the UI changes.

items.title: Create, move, and resize in place
items.body: Drag across open time to create a block, move events between slots and days, and resize directly from the edge where the schedule is visible.

items.title: Recurring events and resource-aware planning
items.body: Events can repeat, resources can define parallel lanes, and the same occurrence model drives selection, editing, and follow-up actions.

items.title: Fits the token system you already use
items.body: The calendar inherits the same shadcn color tokens and exposes className slots, so it can sit inside an existing app instead of forcing a separate visual system.

integration.title: Own the state. Let the calendar handle the surface.
integration.body: CalendarCN is designed as a controlled component. Your app keeps the source of truth for date, view, events, and persistence; the calendar turns that into visible occurrences and interaction callbacks.

flow.title: Keep date, view, and events in your app
flow.body: `useCalendarController` in the demo is only an example. In production, those values can live in React state, a server cache, or whatever store already owns scheduling logic.

flow.title: CalendarRoot expands data into visible occurrences
flow.body: It computes the visible range, expands recurring events for that range, renders the active view, and coordinates drag overlays, selection, keyboard moves, and accessibility announcements.

flow.title: Views stay interchangeable because the model stays stable
flow.body: Switching from a month grid to a time grid or agenda feed does not require a second contract. The same events and resources keep working across every exported view.

flow.title: Mutations come back as typed operations
flow.body: Create, move, resize, duplicate, archive, and delete actions can flow back through handlers you control, so your app decides how changes are validated, persisted, and synchronized.

demo.title: Same calendar behavior, different theme treatment.
demo.body: Switch the site theme from the navbar and the calendar follows along. Layout, event behavior, and view logic stay the same while the tokens around them change.

footer.body: An open-source calendar for shadcn apps, with multiple views, recurring events, resource lanes, and typed scheduling interactions in one reusable surface.

support.label: Buy me a coffee
support.helper: If CalendarCN saved you time, you can help support the project here.

footer.title: CalendarCN
